title: Antrophyum reticulatum
taxonomy: Antrophyum reticulatum is classified under the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This species belongs to the genus Antrophyum, which consists of about 20 species of ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which is a large and diverse group of ferns. The order Polypodiales is also known as the polypody fern order. The classification of Antrophyum reticulatum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.

Its leaves and roots are used in treatments for various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro: Antrophyum reticulatum, also known as the net-veined lady fern or netted lady fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Antrophyum reticulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Antrophyum reticulatum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
